blockReason: Browser MCP unavailable — Chromium-based profiles locked

testCases.title: Payment Form - Field Labels and Placeholders
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Both playwright-edge (msedge-2f58c52) and playwright-chrome (chromium-2f58c52) user-data directories locked — 'Browser is already in use' error on navigate. Another concurrent regression agent in run REG-2026-04-20-1000 is holding both Chromium-based browser profiles. Firefox is the only free engine but is documented (see suite-042 SMK-013/SMK-014) to be unable to access CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe (testflex.cybersource.com) content — would produce a false BLOCKED on every test case that requires filling card fields. Per shared-instructions fallback policy the correct action is to halt and surface the infra blocker rather than run against an unsuitable engine.

testCases.title: Payment Form - Keyboard Tab Order
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Tab-order assertions require entering + exiting CyberSource iframe — Firefox cannot cross that boundary.

testCases.title: Payment Form - Load Time Under 2 Seconds
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Performance timing requires Chrome DevTools MCP or a Chromium-based MCP profile with Network panel access.

testCases.title: Double-Click Place Order Prevention
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Cannot fill CyberSource iframe to reach Place Order with a valid card.

testCases.title: Declined Card - Error Message Displayed
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Cannot submit decline card (4000000000000002) to trigger processor decline response.

testCases.title: Declined Card - Retry with Different Card Succeeds
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: depends on PAY-DECLINE-001 baseline and browser MCP availability (see PAY-UX-001).

testCases.title: Declined Card - Network Timeout Recovery
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Also requires Chrome DevTools MCP Network-block feature to simulate the timeout.

testCases.title: Declined Card - Double-Click Prevention After Error
Declined Card - Cart and Checkout State Preserved
Guest Checkout - CyberSource Payment Completes Without Account
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Guest checkout also requires a fresh incognito/private context which needs a clean Chromium profile.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Payment Validation Same as Authenticated User
Cross-Layer - Order Total at Checkout Matches Gateway Charge Amount
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: browser MCP unavailable (see PAY-UX-001). Also needs DevTools Network tab to inspect CyberSource tokenization request 'amount' field — only reachable via Chromium-based profile.

testCases.title: Declined Card Recovery - CyberSource Error Then Retry With Valid Card
Multiple Consecutive Declines Preserve Checkout State

errors: INFRA: playwright-edge MCP returned 'Browser is already in use for C:\Users\mutyk\AppData\Local\ms-playwright\mcp-msedge-2f58c52, use --isolated to run multiple instances of the same browser' on every navigate attempt.
INFRA: playwright-chrome MCP returned the same error for mcp-chromium-2f58c52. Both Chromium-based user-data directories held by a concurrent agent in run REG-2026-04-20-1000.

INFRA: playwright-firefox is the only free engine but is documented to be unable to read/interact with CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe (testflex.cybersource.com) — see suite-042-results.json SMK-013/SMK-014 BLOCKED rationale. Running Suite 041 against Firefox would produce 14 BLOCKED (same root cause) rather than real PASS/FAIL signal, so execution was halted without a browser per shared-instructions fallback policy.
BUG-CVV-plain-text retest: NOT VERIFIED — requires Chromium-based MCP to inspect Microform iframe boundary + DevTools Network tab for tokenization payload. Verdict pending.
PCI compliance iframe assertions: NOT VERIFIED — same root cause as BUG-CVV-plain-text retest.
retryRecommendation: Retry with --isolated flag on playwright-edge OR wait for concurrent agent holding mcp-msedge-2f58c52/mcp-chromium-2f58c52 profiles to release them, then re-run suite-041. ALL 14 test cases need a Chromium-based engine (edge OR chrome) — firefox cannot satisfy the CyberSource Microform iframe assertions that this suite is built around (per manifest browserRequirementReason). If the conflict persists, escalate to regression-orchestrator to serialize suite-041 with suite-039 (both CyberSource-dependent).
